summary: runCLISubprocess captures stdout and stderr into separate buffers (replacing cmd.CombinedOutput) so intent handlers parse pure JSON stdout without the root PersistentPostRunE's stderr quota line corrupting trailing bytes.
reason: cmd.CombinedOutput() merged the subprocess CLI's stderr into its stdout. The root PersistentPostRunE always writes `[quota] X/1000 used, Y left` to stderr after every command, so handleBatchWithQuotaGuard and handlePopScarcityReport were json.Unmarshal-ing `{...}\n[quota] ...\n` and silently swallowing the trailing-content error, leaving `result["forecast"]` and scarcity-bucket data as nil. New behavior pipes stderr into a separate buffer and surfaces it in the wrapped error on failure. Greptile P1 finding on PR #630 (review 7).
